page: Microsoft Azure EC32as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ECACCV5 family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The hardware configuration features 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing hyperthreading with 2.0 threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 support. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across various workloads. The server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and strong image processing capabilities in Geekbench, both placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also a highlight, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth (both cached and uncached), and software compilation speeds fall within the average range. For database operations, the server achieves average scores in PassMark database tests and Redis SET operations.

In language model inference tests, the instance shows strong text generation speeds for small 135M models, but performance drops to average for medium 7B models and becomes weak for large 70B models. This indicates a clear tradeoff when handling larger parameter models without dedicated GPU acceleration. Given its memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6 GB per vCPU and its strong memory performance, the EC32as_cc_v5 is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure EC32as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 server instance in the ECACCV5 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 192.0 GB of memory, yielding 6.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory scores, both placing in the top 25%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. The server exhibits strong text generation speeds for small language models, but performance degrades to weak for larger 70B models. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC32as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 server featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor and 192 GB of memory. It delivers strong single-core and memory performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC32as_cc_v5 is a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 192 GB of RAM, offering strong single-core and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring thirty-two vCPUs and high memory capacity for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory providing 6.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Suitable for memory-intensive workloads, databases, and general-purpose applications
